articles.headline: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
articles.description: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

images.name: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
images.caption: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: England sign off WC prep with 3-0 win as Rice, Gordon, Watkins score
articles.description: Returning Declan Rice set England on course for a morale-boosting 3-0 victory against Costa Rica after a heavy thunderstorm in Orlando delayed their final World Cup warmup match by an hour.

images.name: Declan Rice [1296x729]
images.caption: Declan Rice opened the scoring for England shortly after 10 minutes played.

images.name: Tuchel: England can't be World Cup favourites
images.caption: Thomas Tuchel says England can't be favourites because there are "proven winners" at the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: Burley questions if Portugal's Martinez would drop Ronaldo
articles.description: Craig Burley debates whether Roberto Martinez should drop Cristiano Ronaldo amid growing scrutiny over his place in Portugal's starting lineup.

images.name: Burley questions if Portugal's Martinez would drop Ronaldo
images.caption: Craig Burley debates whether Roberto Martinez should drop Cristiano Ronaldo amid growing scrutiny over his place in Portugal's starting lineup.

articles.headline: Argentina wins as Messi scores in cameo in front of 88k in Alabama
articles.description: Argentine legend Messi, who is working his way back from muscle fatigue ahead of the start of the FIFA World Cup, came on in the 70th minute and scored from the penalty spot right after in his team's 3-0 win over Iceland.

images.name: Messi celeb Arg Iceland_260609 [1296x729]
images.caption: Lionel Messi celebrates after scoring a goal for Argentina in a friendly against Iceland.
images.alt: Lionel Messi celebrates after scoring a goal for Argentina in a friendly against Iceland.

article.headline: MLS-heavy USMNT side claims win over Venezuela
article.description: A heavily-rotated United States men's national team earned a 3-1 victory over Venezuela in the first of two international friendlies this month.
article.linkText: MLS-heavy USMNT side claims win over Venezuela

article.story:
A heavily-rotated United States men's national team earned a 3-1 victory over Venezuela in the first of its two international friendlies this month.

Saturday's U.S. squad was made up almost entirely of players from Major League Soccer due to the game falling outside of a FIFA international window.

Jack McGlynn and Patrick Agyemang scored about two minutes apart late in the first half and Matko Miljevic, whose third-minute penalty kick was saved, struck in the 64th minute.

McGlynn connected on a left-footed strike from about 30 yards in the 37th minute, Agyemang deflected a shot off goalkeeper Wuilker Faríñez not long afterward and the Americans were off and running. Milijevic scored on a counter, joining Agyemang to raise the total to 61 players who scored in their U.S. debuts.

The result means manager Mauricio Pochettino has now won four of his five games since he took charge in September -- his lone defeat came in a friendly against Mexico in October.

The USMNT will be in action again when it faces Costa Rica on Wednesday.

DaMarcus Beasley, Jozy Altidore, Clint Dempsey, DeAndre Yedlin and Landon Donovan are among the players whose national team careers were boosted by January camps.

The U.S. controlled much of the match and had a chance to grab the lead after Daniel Pereira's foul in the penalty area. Miljevic, a Miami-born forward, didn't have much on the spot kick he tried pushing past Faríñez's left side.

Six U.S. starters made debuts before a crowd of 18,008: left back Max Arfsten, central defender George Campbell, wingers Brian Gutierrez and Carden Clark, along with Miljevic and Agyemang at forward. Emeka Eneli and Indiana Vassilev made debuts in the second half.

Information from the Associated Press contributed to this report.

keyEvents.text: First Half begins.
Penalty saved. Matko Miljevic (USA) right footed shot saved in the centre of the goal.
Benjamín Cremaschi (USA) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Goal! USA 1, Venezuela 0. Jack McGlynn (USA) left footed shot from outside the box to the top right corner. Assisted by Caden Clark.
Goal! USA 2, Venezuela 0. Patrick Agyemang (USA) left footed shot from the left side of the box to the centre of the goal. Assisted by Matko Miljevic following a fast break.
Substitution, Venezuela. Edson Tortolero replaces Jovanny Bolívar.
Substitution, Venezuela. Jorge Yriarte replaces Daniel Pereira.
Substitution, Venezuela. Saúl Guarirapa replaces Jan Hurtado.
Substitution, Venezuela. Maurice Cova replaces Juan Añor.
Substitution, Venezuela. Thomas Gutiérrez replaces Francisco La Mantia.
Goal! USA 3, Venezuela 0. Matko Miljevic (USA) right footed shot from outside the box to the bottom left corner. Assisted by Jack McGlynn following a fast break.
Substitution, USA. Emeka Eneli replaces Benjamín Cremaschi.
Substitution, USA. Brian White replaces Patrick Agyemang.
Substitution, USA. DeJuan Jones replaces Max Arfsten.
Substitution, USA. Diego Luna replaces Matko Miljevic.
Goal! USA 3, Venezuela 1. Jorge Yriarte (Venezuela) header from the centre of the box to the top left corner. Assisted by Edson Tortolero with a cross following a corner.
Substitution, USA. Indiana Vassilev replaces Brian Gutiérrez.
Substitution, Venezuela. Erickson Gallardo replaces Bryant Ortega.
Substitution, USA. Walker Zimmerman replaces George Campbell.
Shaq Moore (USA) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Second Half ends, USA 3, Venezuela 1.
